title: Lively Algerian Chickpea Tagine
description: A vibrant low-glycemic tagine with chickpeas, rosemary and a zesty yuzu-kosho finish that awakens the senses.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a tagine or heavy pot over medium heat and sauté onion and garlic until fragrant, about 3 minutes.
Add sliced carrots and zucchini and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in diced tomatoes, chickpeas, fresh rosemary, salt and fermented soy sauce, then bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Cover the pot, reduce heat to low, and let the tagine cook for 25 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
Remove the lid, stir in yuzu-kosho and lemon juice and adjust seasoning with more salt if desired.
Taste and ensure the internal temperature reaches 70°C to ensure safety, then remove from heat.
Let the tagine rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to settle.
Garnish with chopped parsley or cilantro if desired, and serve hot.
